# Recycler — Copilot Instructions

## Build & Lint (Required Before Finishing)

Always run both of the following before marking a task complete. Fix any errors before responding.
Use `make` targets where they exist, falling back to the raw commands if a target is unavailable.

```bash
make build        # fallback: go build ./...
make lint         # fallback: ./bin/golangci-lint run ./...
```

## Manifests & Helm (Required After API or Config Changes)

After any change to `api/v1alpha1/` types or kubebuilder marker annotations, regenerate CRDs and deepcopy methods:

```bash
make generate manifests
```

Always run `make build && make lint` after the above.

The Helm chart (`helm-charts/recycler/`) is **hand-maintained**. Do not run `make helm` as part of normal development — it overwrites the chart with helmify output and will clobber hand-crafted templates and values. `make helm` exists only as a reference bootstrap tool.

After any structural change to `config/` (new resource type, RBAC change, new deployment arg), manually mirror the equivalent change in `helm-charts/recycler/templates/` and `helm-charts/recycler/values.yaml`.

After any change to `api/v1alpha1/` types that adds, removes, or modifies a field (including doc comment changes that affect CRD descriptions), run `make generate manifests` and then manually mirror the updated field schema into **both** `helm-charts/recycler/templates/recycler-crd.yaml` and `helm-charts/recycler-debug/templates/recycler-crd.yaml`. The hand-maintained Helm CRDs are the only CRD copy that does **not** auto-update — omitting this step causes "unknown field" warnings in production when the Helm-installed CRD schema is missing fields that the controller or stored CRs already use.

## What the Operator Does

Recycler monitors CPU utilization of pods in a target Deployment and automatically terminates pods whose rolling-average CPU exceeds a configured threshold. It solves runaway pods that evade health checks and avoids unnecessary HPA scaling.

Two controllers work together:
- **MonitorReconciler** — determines per-pod CPU utilization and annotates pods that breach the threshold. With `metricsSource: kubernetes` (default) it polls the Kubernetes Metrics API on a configurable interval and builds a rolling history of CPU datapoints per pod; with `metricsSource: prometheus` it queries an external Prometheus server (no per-pod history is stored, averaging is done by the PromQL query).
- **RecyclerReconciler** — watches for breach annotations and deletes the pod after a configurable delay, respecting a grace period.

## Project Layout

```
api/v1alpha1/          CRD types — run `make generate manifests` after any change
cmd/main.go            Manager entrypoint (no custom changes needed for metrics)
internal/controller/
  recycler_controller.go   Termination logic
  monitor_controller.go    CPU polling & threshold detection (kubernetes + prometheus sources)
  prometheus.go            Prometheus query client (PromQL template, instant-vector parsing)
  metrics.go               Custom Prometheus metrics (registered via init())
config/
  overlays/debug/      deploy-debug overlay (zap-log-level=1, zap-devel=true)
  overlays/production/ production overlay (replicas=2, higher limits)
  default/             Base kustomization (namespace: recycler-system, namePrefix: recycler-)
test/e2e/
  e2e_test.go          Ginkgo e2e suite
  testdata/            cpu-stress-deployment.yaml, cpu-stress-recycler.yaml,
                       cpu-stress-recycler-prometheus.yaml, prometheus-stack.yaml
test/utils/utils.go    kubectl helpers, FetchControllerMetrics, MetricValue, QueryPrometheus
helm-charts/recycler/  Helm chart generated by helmify from config/default
```

## API Types (`api/v1alpha1/recycler_types.go`)

| Field | Type | Default | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|---------|
| `scaleTargetRef.kind` | string | `"Deployment"` | Only `Deployment` is supported |
| `scaleTargetRef.name` | string | required | Name of the target Deployment |
| `scaleTargetRef.apiVersion` | string | `"apps/v1"` | API version of target |
| `averageCpuUtilizationPercent` | int32 | required | CPU % threshold to trigger breach |
| `recycleDelaySeconds` | int32 | `300` | Seconds to wait after breach before deleting |
| `pollingIntervalSeconds` | int32 | `60` | Seconds between metrics polls |
| `podMetricsHistory` | int32 | `10` | Rolling window size (datapoints) |
| `gracePeriodSeconds` | int32 | `30` | Pod deletion grace period |
| `metricStorageLocation` | string | `"memory"` | `"memory"` or `"annotation"` (only used when `metricsSource` is `"kubernetes"`). **No `omitempty`** — must always be set explicitly when constructing a Recycler in Go (see note below). |
| `metricsSource` | string | `"kubernetes"` | `"kubernetes"` (poll Metrics API + rolling average) or `"prometheus"` (query external Prometheus) |
| `prometheus` | `*PrometheusSpec` | nil | External Prometheus config; **required** when `metricsSource` is `"prometheus"`, ignored otherwise |
| `prometheus.serverAddress` | string | required | Base URL of the Prometheus server, e.g. `http://prometheus-operated.monitoring.svc:9090` |
| `prometheus.query` | string | default cAdvisor-only query (no kube-state-metrics dependency) | PromQL template; must return an instant vector with a `pod` label and a CPU % value. Template fields: `{{.Namespace}}`, `{{.Deployment}}`, `{{.PodRegex}}`, `{{.WindowSeconds}}` |
| `prometheus.insecureSkipVerify` | bool | `false` | Disable TLS cert verification for an HTTPS `serverAddress` |
| `metricsRetentionSeconds` | int32 | `300` | How long to retain `recycler_pod_last_recycle_timestamp_seconds` after pod deletion before removing the series |

A CEL validation on the spec enforces that `prometheus` is set when `metricsSource: prometheus`. `metricsSource` uses `json:"metricsSource,omitempty"` so an unset value is omitted and the CRD default (`kubernetes`) applies — do not drop `omitempty` or explicit empty strings will fail the enum.

### Enum fields with defaults — `omitempty` rule (read before changing enum tags or writing fixtures)

A `+kubebuilder:validation:Enum` string field with a `+kubebuilder:default` only gets the default applied by the API server when the field is **absent** from the submitted JSON. A Go zero value `""` is **not** absent unless the field has `omitempty` — it marshals as an explicit empty string and fails the enum before the default can apply (HTTP 422 `Unsupported value: ""`).

The two enum-with-default fields are currently inconsistent and MUST be treated differently:

| Field | JSON tag | Consequence |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `metricsSource` | `metricsSource,omitempty` | Safe to leave unset in Go — the `kubernetes` default applies. |
| `metricStorageLocation` | `metricStorageLocation` (no `omitempty`) | Leaving it unset on a Recycler you `Create` fails validation. **Always set it explicitly** (e.g. `MetricStorageLocation: StorageMemory`), even in `metricsSource: prometheus` mode where it is ignored. |

**Whenever you change either field (or add a new enum-with-default field), keep these two in sync:** the JSON tag in `api/v1alpha1/recycler_types.go`, the regenerated CRD enum/default (`make generate manifests`), and every Go test/sample fixture that `Create`s a Recycler. If you add `omitempty`, fixtures may omit the field; if you do not, every fixture that persists a Recycler must set it. Prefer adding `omitempty` to new enum-with-default fields so fixtures and real CRs can omit them safely.

Status uses `[]metav1.Condition` with types `"Available"` / `"Unavailable"`.

After changing types, always run:
```bash
make generate manifests
```

## Key Constants — Reuse, Never Redefine

```go
// recycler_controller.go
recyclerFinalizer         = "recycler.k8s.io/recycler"
recyclerControllerName    = "recycler"          // also used as Prometheus label value
typeHealthyCondition      = "Available"
typeUnhealthyCondition    = "Unavailable"
cpuBreachTimestampAnnotation = "recycler.theonlyway.com/cpu-breach-timestamp"
podMetricsAnnotation         = "recycler.theonlyway.com/pod-metrics-history"

// monitor_controller.go
monitorControllerName = "monitor"
StorageMemory         = "memory"
StorageAnnotation     = "annotation"
kindDeployment        = "Deployment"
MetricsSourceKubernetes = "kubernetes"
MetricsSourcePrometheus = "prometheus"

// metrics.go
labelNamespace = "namespace"
labelPod       = "pod"
```

Event reasons: `"PodTerminated"`, `"CPUThresholdBreached"`, `"CPUThresholdRecovered"`, `"AnnotationsRemoved"`, `"FinalizerError"`.

## Controller Conventions

- **All status/annotation writes** use `retry.RetryOnConflict(retry.DefaultRetry, func() error { ... })` — re-fetch the object inside the retry closure before updating.
- **Requeue** by returning `ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: time.Duration(recycler.Spec.PollingIntervalSeconds) * time.Second}`.
- **Events** are recorded to both the pod and the Recycler CR via `r.Recorder.Eventf(obj, relatedObj, ...)`.
- **In-memory storage** uses the exported `InMemoryMetricsStorage` (`sync.Map`), key format `"namespace/podname"`, value type `[]PodCPUUsage`. Clean up on pod deletion and CR finalizer.

## Storage Modes

Storage modes only apply when `metricsSource: kubernetes`. With `metricsSource: prometheus` no per-pod history is stored — `reconcilePrometheusMetrics` queries Prometheus each reconcile and `evaluatePodThreshold` (shared by both sources) applies the breach/recovery logic on the returned value.

| | `memory` (default) | `annotation` |
|---|---|---|
| Where | `InMemoryMetricsStorage` sync.Map | Pod annotation (JSON) |
| Key / annotation | `"namespace/podname"` | `podMetricsAnnotation` |
| Survives restart | No | Yes |
| Write cost | Zero | etcd write per poll |
| Cleanup | Delete key on pod delete / CR finalizer | Remove annotation on CR finalizer |

## Custom Prometheus Metrics (`internal/controller/metrics.go`)

All registered to `metrics.Registry` (controller-runtime's global registry — the `/metrics` endpoint). No `cmd/main.go` changes needed.

| Metric | Type | Labels | Updated when |
|--------|------|--------|-------------|
| `recycler_pod_recycles_total` | CounterVec | `recycler_namespace`, `recycler` | Pod successfully deleted in `terminatePods()` |
| `recycler_cpu_breach_duration_seconds` | HistogramVec | `recycler_namespace`, `recycler` | Same — observes `elapsed.Seconds()` |
| `recycler_cpu_threshold_breaches_total` | CounterVec | `recycler_namespace`, `recycler` | Breach annotation first written in `handleThresholdBreach()` |
| `recycler_pod_last_recycle_timestamp_seconds` | GaugeVec | `recycler_namespace`, `recycler`, `recycler_pod` | Pod successfully deleted in `terminatePods()` via `SetToCurrentTime()` |
| `recycler_pod_cpu_utilization_percent` | GaugeVec | `recycler_namespace`, `recycler_pod` | Every reconcile in `checkPodMetricsAnnotation()` |

Label name constants `labelNamespace`, `labelPod`, and `recyclerControllerName` are already defined — use them, don't re-declare.

### Metric Series Cleanup

Per-pod gauge series must be explicitly deleted when a pod is no longer relevant — Prometheus will otherwise retain stale series indefinitely.

| Metric | When to delete | How |
|--------|---------------|-----|
| `recycler_pod_cpu_utilization_percent` | Immediately on pod deletion in `terminatePods()` | `podCPUUtilization.DeleteLabelValues(ns, pod)` |
| `recycler_pod_last_recycle_timestamp_seconds` | 5 minutes after pod deletion (via `time.AfterFunc`) to allow at least one Prometheus scrape | `podLastRecycleTime.DeleteLabelValues(ns, recycler, pod)` |
| `recycler_pod_cpu_utilization_percent` | Immediately in finalizer (`doFinalizerOperationsForRecycler`) when CR is deleted | `podCPUUtilization.DeleteLabelValues(ns, pod)` |
| `recycler_pod_last_recycle_timestamp_seconds` | Immediately in finalizer when CR is deleted (value was already scraped long ago) | `podLastRecycleTime.DeleteLabelValues(ns, recycler, pod)` |

Counter (`recycler_pod_recycles_total`, `recycler_cpu_threshold_breaches_total`) and histogram (`recycler_cpu_breach_duration_seconds`) series are keyed by namespace+recycler only — no per-pod label, no cleanup needed.

## Build & Make Targets

```bash
make build              # compile to bin/manager
make manifests          # regenerate CRD YAML from kubebuilder annotations
make generate           # regenerate DeepCopy methods
make install            # kubectl apply CRDs to current cluster
make deploy             # kustomize build config/default | kubectl apply
make deploy-debug       # kustomize build config/overlays/debug | kubectl apply
make undeploy           # remove controller from cluster
make test               # unit tests with coverage (uses envtest)
make test-e2e           # create Kind cluster + run e2e + teardown
make setup-test-e2e     # create Kind cluster only (recycler-operator-test-e2e)
make cleanup-test-e2e   # delete Kind cluster
make helm               # regenerate helm-charts/recycler/ from config/default
make lint               # golangci-lint check
make lint-fix           # golangci-lint --fix
make docker-buildx      # multi-arch build; pass PLATFORM= and BUILD_MODE=load|push
```

Binaries live in `bin/`: `golangci-lint`, `controller-gen`, `kustomize`, `helmify`, `setup-envtest`, `jq`, `yq`.
Module path: `github.com/theonlyway/recycler`.

## E2E Tests

Tests run against a Kind cluster (`recycler-operator-test-e2e`). `BeforeAll` installs:
- Prometheus Operator v0.86.0
- cert-manager v1.19.1
- metrics-server v0.8.0 (patched for Kind: `--kubelet-insecure-tls`, `--metric-resolution=10s`)

**Test flow** (`e2e_test.go`):
1. Build image → `make docker-buildx ... BUILD_MODE=load`
2. `kind load docker-image`
3. `make install` + `make deploy-debug`
4. Verify controller pod running in `recycler-system`
5. Deploy CPU stress workload in `cpu-test` namespace
6. Apply Recycler CR, wait for `Available` status
7. Wait for all initial pods to be terminated (timeout calculated from CR config)
8. Assert Kubernetes events (`PodTerminated`, `CPUThresholdBreached`)
9. Assert custom Prometheus metrics via `utils.FetchControllerMetrics` + `utils.MetricValue`
10. Verify replacement pods are running

**Key utils** (`test/utils/utils.go`):
- `Run(cmd)` — executes in project root, logs to GinkgoWriter
- `FetchControllerMetrics(namespace)` — port-forwards `recycler-controller-manager-metrics-service:8443` → `localhost:18443`, creates a short-lived service account token, GETs `/metrics` over HTTPS (InsecureSkipVerify — test-only), returns raw Prometheus text
- `MetricValue(body, metricName, labels)` — parses Prometheus text format, partial label match, returns `(float64, bool)`
- `LoadImageToKindClusterWithName(name)` — respects `KIND_CLUSTER` env var

## Config & Deployment

Kustomize namePrefix `recycler-` is applied to all resources. The service name for metrics is therefore `recycler-controller-manager-metrics-service` and the service account is `recycler-controller-manager`.

Metrics endpoint is HTTPS on `:8443` with token authentication (`secureMetrics=true` default). In tests, access it via port-forward + `kubectl create token`.

**RBAC the operator needs**: pods (delete/get/list/patch/update/watch), deployments (get/list/watch), events (create/patch), metrics.k8s.io/pods (get/list), recyclers + subresources (full CRUD).

## Helm Chart

Generated from `config/default` via `make helm`. Do not hand-edit `helm-charts/recycler/templates/` — regenerate instead. Key values: `controllerManager.recycler.image.repository`, `controllerManager.replicas`, `metricsService.ports`.
